conjecture_statement: Let the Watson integral for the 3-dimensional simple cubic lattice be defined as $W_3 := \frac{1}{\pi^3} \int_{[0, \pi]^3} \frac{1}{3 - \sum_{i=1}^3 \cos(k_i)} d\mathbf{k}$. This integral converges and satisfies the identity $W_3 = \int_0^\infty e^{-3t} I_0(t)^3 dt$, where $I_0(t) = \frac{1}{\pi} \int_0^\pi e^{t \cos\theta} d\theta$ is the modified Bessel function of the first kind.

import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Bessel
import Mathlib.Analysis.Integration.IntegralTransforms
import Mathlib.Analysis.Fourier.FourierTransform
import Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix
import Mathlib.Topology.Instances.Real
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Integral.Bochner

open MeasureTheory Filter Topology Real Set Nat

noncomputable section

-- Definition of the Modified Bessel function I₀ for the purpose of the sketch.
def modifiedBesselI0 (t : ℝ) : ℝ := (1/π) * ∫ x in 0..π, exp (t * cos x)

-- Definition of the Watson Integral for the simple cubic lattice.
def watson_integral_3d : ℝ := (1/π^3) * ∫ k₁ in 0..π, ∫ k₂ in 0..π, ∫ k₃ in 0..π, (3 - cos k₁ - cos k₂ - cos k₃)⁻¹

-- Definition of the equivalent Bessel function representation.
def bessel_representation_3d : ℝ := ∫ t in 0..∞, exp (-3 * t) * (modifiedBesselI0 t)^3

-- Conjecture: The two representations are equal.
theorem w6_watson_integral : watson_integral_3d = bessel_representation_3d := by
  -- The proof relates both expressions to the generating function for the probability of a random
  -- walk on ℤ³ returning to the origin. Let G(1) be the expected number of returns.
  let P_return : ℕ → ℝ := sorry -- Probability of return to origin at step n.
  let G (z : ℂ) : ℂ := ∑' n, (P_return n) • z ^ n

  -- Sub-Lemma 1: The lattice walk generates a recurrence.
  -- The coefficients P_return(2n) of the generating function G(z) are determined by the
  -- number of closed paths of length 2n on the ℤ³ lattice.
  have h_walk : ∀ n, P_return (2 * n) > 0 ∧ P_return (2 * n + 1) = 0 := by

lean4_sketch_archimedes:
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Bessel
import Mathlib.Analysis.Integration
import Mathlib.Analysis.Fourier.FourierTransform
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.Basic
import Mathlib.Topology.Instances.Real
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Integral.Bochner
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Fresnel
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Integrals

open MeasureTheory Filter Topology Real Set Nat Complex

noncomputable section

-- Definition of the Modified Bessel function I₀ for the purpose of the sketch.
def modifiedBesselI0 (t : ℝ) : ℝ := real.bessel_i 0 t

-- Definition of the Watson Integral for the simple cubic lattice.
def watson_integral_3d : ℝ := (1/π^3) * ∫ k₁ in (0)..π, ∫ k₂ in (0)..π, ∫ k₃ in (0)..π, (3 - cos k₁ - cos k₂ - cos k₃)⁻¹

-- Definition of the equivalent Bessel function representation.
def bessel_representation_3d : ℝ := ∫ t in (0)..∞, exp (-3 * t) * (modifiedBesselI0 t)^3

-- Conjecture: The two representations are equal.
theorem w6_watson_integral : watson_integral_3d = bessel_representation_3d := by
  -- The proof relates both expressions to the generating function for the probability of a random
  -- walk on ℤ³ returning to the origin. Let G(1) be the expected number of returns.
  let P_return : ℕ → ℝ := fun n => if n % 2 = 0 then
    (1/(2*π)^3) * ∫ k₁ in (-π)..π, ∫ k₂ in (-π)..π, ∫ k₃ in (-π)..π,
      ((cos k₁ + cos k₂ + cos k₃)/3)^n
    else 0

  let G (z : ℂ) : ℂ := ∑' n, (P_return n) * z ^ n

  -- Sub-Lemma 1: The lattice walk generates a recurrence.
  have h_walk : ∀ n, P_return (2 * n) > 0 ∧ P_return (2 * n + 1) = 0 := by
    intro n
    constructor
    · unfold P_return
      split
      · apply integral_pos_of_nonneg
        intro x _
        apply pow_nonneg
        apply div_nonneg
        · apply add_nonneg
          all_goals apply cos_nonneg_of_mem_Icc
          all_goals linarith
        · norm_num
      · norm_num
    · unfold P_return
      simp only [Nat.odd_add, not_false_eq_true, Nat.odd_mul, Nat.even_add_one, not_true_eq_false,
        ↓reduceIte]

  -- Sub-Lemma 2: The partition function integral representation.
  have h_G_eq : G 1 = watson_integral_3d := by
    unfold G watson_integral_3d P_return
    rw [tsum_eq_sum]
    · simp only [ite_mul, zero_mul, one_mul, sum_ite_eq, sum_const_zero, sum_filter]
      rw [← sum_add_tsum_geometric_of_norm_le_one (by norm_num)]
      · congr 1
        ext n
        rw [integral_eq_integral_of_le (by linarith)]
        congr 1
        ext k₁ k₂ k₃
        rw [div_pow, ← mul_pow, ← mul_add]
        congr 1
        ring_nf
      · intro b hb
        apply norm_le_pow_of_pow_le_one (by norm_num)
        apply div_le_one_of_le (by norm_num)
        apply le_trans (add_le_add_left (add_le_add_left (cos_le_one _) _) _)
        apply add_le_add
        · apply cos_le_one
        · apply cos_le_one
    · intro n
      apply summable_pow_mul_geometric_of_norm_lt (by norm_num)
      apply div_lt_one_of_lt (by norm_num)
      apply lt_of_le_of_lt (add_le_add_left (add_le_add_left (neg_one_le_cos _) _) _)
      apply add_lt_add_of_lt_of_le
      · apply lt_of_le_of_lt (neg_one_le_cos _) (cos_lt_one _)
      · apply le_trans (add_le_add_left (le_abs_self _) _)
        apply add_le_add
        · apply le_abs_self
        · apply le_abs_self

  -- Sub-Lemma 3: The Bessel function representation.
  have h_bessel_eq : G 1 = bessel_representation_3d := by
    unfold G bessel_representation_3d P_return modifiedBesselI0
    rw [tsum_eq_sum]
    · simp only [ite_mul, zero_mul, one_mul, sum_ite_eq, sum_const_zero, sum_filter]
      rw [← sum_add_tsum_geometric_of_norm_le_one (by norm_num)]
      · congr 1
        ext n
        rw [integral_eq_integral_of_le (by linarith)]
        congr 1
        ext k₁ k₂ k₃
        rw [div_pow, ← mul_pow, ← mul_add, real.bessel_i_zero, real.bessel_i_zero, real.bessel_i_zero]
        simp only [mul_pow, Real.exp_add, Real.exp_mul, Real.exp_neg]
        congr 1
        rw [← integral_cos_pow_eq_bessel_i]
        · congr 1
          ring_nf
        · linarith
      · intro b hb
        apply norm_le_pow_of_pow_le_one (by norm_num)
        apply div_le_one_of_le (by norm_num)
        apply le_trans (add_le_add_left (add_le_add_left (cos_le_one _) _) _)
        apply add_le_add
        · apply cos_le_one
        · apply cos_le_one
    · intro n
      apply summable_pow_mul_geometric_of_norm_lt (by norm_num)
      apply div_lt_one_of_lt (by norm_num)
      apply lt_of_le_of_lt (add_le_add_left (add_le_add_left (neg_one_le_cos _) _) _)
      apply add_lt_add_of_lt_of_le
      · apply lt_of_le_of_lt (neg_one_le_cos _) (cos_lt_one _)
      · apply le_trans (add_le_add_left (le_abs_self _) _)
        apply add_le_add
        · apply le_abs_self
        · apply le_abs_self

  rw [h_G_eq, h_bessel_eq]
